codeReview.summary: I reviewed the prior failed review, both feature handoffs, the fix transcript skeleton, both feature diffs (`d8704f6` and `764f697`), the current codegen/typecheck paths, and a targeted three-level nested `Option` compile. `764f697` does fix the one-level wildcard/default-fallback gaps called out in the earlier review, but the implementation is still not fully recursive: typecheck still accepts deeper built-in nesting, while codegen still only unwraps one inner built-in layer and falls back to generated panic paths for legal deeper matches.

issues.description: `extract_inner_builtin_pat` still peels only a single inner built-in layer, and all nested bind/wildcard branches only accept the exact one-level shapes at `lib/codegen.ml:2434-2449`, `2497-2514`, `2596-2612`, `2656-2667`, `2680-2687`, `2729-2747`, `2801-2814`, and `2832-2841`. Meanwhile `bind_pattern` in `lib/typecheck.ml:1467-1476` remains recursively permissive for built-in `Option`/`Result` patterns, so deeper legal Copy-payload matches are still accepted. A concrete repro compiled successfully today: `Option::Some(Option::Some(Option::Some(v))) => println(v), _ => println("fallback")` lowered to Go containing `panic("unsupported nested pattern")` inside the generated inner branch instead of recursively binding `v` or reaching the fallback arm. That means the fix still does not satisfy the feature's requirement that every accepted nested built-in Copy-payload pattern lower without generated panic paths.

summary: Fail. The fix commit addresses the prior one-level wildcard/default-fallback bug, but built-in pattern codegen is still not fully recursive: deeper accepted nested `Option`/`Result` patterns still lower to generated `panic("unsupported nested pattern")` paths, so the original failure is only partially resolved.
